_provenance.extracted_by: in-session Claude Code sub-agent (no API call), 2026-06-22
_provenance.note: Predicate-calculus description groups lifted from prose. The structural crux: riddles 1&2 use DIVIDES (per-person = total/count); riddles 3&4 use EQUALS (per-person = the whole total). Near-identical surface, different relational structure -> different answers. Every symbol is grounded to a verbatim source substring; verified deterministically by grounding/verify.py.

source.text: Suppose it took 3 people a total of 300 dollars to finish a job. If the rate of the three was the same, then we know that each person spent 100 dollars on the job.

target.text: Suppose it took 3 people a total of 3 hours to finish a job. If the rate of the three was the same, then we know that each person spent ___ on the job.

source.text: Suppose it took 3 people a total of 300 dollars to finish a job. If the rate of the three was the same, then we know that it took each person 100 dollars to finish the job.

target.text: Suppose it took 3 people a total of 3 hours to finish a job. If the rate of the three was the same, then we know that it took each person ___ to finish the job.

source.text: Suppose it took 3 people a total of 300 dollars to finish a job. If the rate of the three was the same, then we know that it took each person 300 dollars to finish the same job.

target.text: Suppose it took 3 people a total of 3 hours to finish a job. If the rate of the three was the same, then we know that it took each person ___ to finish the same job.

source.text: Suppose it took 3 people a total of 300 dollars to finish a job. If the rate of the three was the same, then we know that it took one person 300 dollars to finish the same job.

target.text: Suppose it took 3 people a total of 3 hours to finish a job. If the rate of the three was the same, then we know that it took one person ___ to finish the same job.
